---
title: "ex14. Causal (Different backends, SB Backend) - Amoroso Kernel"
---




> **Website workflow note.**
> This page reflects the current exported API and recommended wrapper-first usage.
> Last updated: **2026-02-19**.
>
> For the full package narrative, see the main package vignettes (basic, unconditional, conditional, and causal).


# Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel {#causal-different-backends-gpd}

This vignette fits two mixed-backend causal models with GPD tails and an Amoroso kernel:

- Model A: Treated = SB, Control = CRP
- Model B: Treated = CRP, Control = SB

We shift y to keep outcomes in a stable positive range.

## What you’ll learn

- How to fit mixed-backend causal models when outcomes are shifted positive and modeled with **Amoroso** bulk + **GPD tails**.
- How tail augmentation influences survival/quantile predictions and downstream effect summaries.
- How to keep model comparisons interpretable by reusing the same causal S3 workflow.
## When to use this template

- Outcomes are (or can be made) positive and you need a bulk family like Amoroso plus an explicit extreme-tail model.
- You want a stress-test style causal analysis where **extremes matter** and may differ by treatment arm.
## Key limitation (important)

Shifting outcomes changes the scale: interpret effects on the shifted scale (or transform back carefully if you need original-scale statements).
## Next steps

- Compare against the bulk-only mixed-backend setup to isolate the incremental impact of `GPD = TRUE`.
---

## Data Setup


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2")
y_raw <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y
A <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$A
X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)

shift <- abs(min(y_raw)) + 0.1
y <- y_raw + shift

summary_tbl <- tibble(
  statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),
  value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y))
)

summary_tbl
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 5 × 2
  statistic    value
  <chr>        <dbl>
1 N         500     
2 Mean        8.46  
3 SD          1.76  
4 Min         0.1000
5 Max        13.5   
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE]
y_eval <- y[1:40]
u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE))
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, A = as.factor(A), x1 = X[, 1])

p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = A)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Shifted Outcome vs X1", newdata ="X1", y = "y (shifted)", color = "Treatment") +
  theme_minimal()

p_scatter
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](ex14-causal-different-backends-sb_files/figure-html/data-scatter-1.png){width=768}
:::
:::


---

## Model A: Treated SB, Control CRP (Amoroso + GPD)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
param_specs_gpd <- list(
  gpd = list(
    threshold = list(
      mode = "dist",
      dist = "lognormal",
      # Use max() to guard against log(0) = -Inf by enforcing a positive lower bound.
      args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)
    )
  )
)

bundle_sb_crp <- bundle(
  x = y,
  treat = A,
  X = X,
  kernel = c("amoroso", "amoroso"),
  backend = c("sb", "crp"),
  PS = "logit",
  GPD = c(TRUE, TRUE),
  components = c(5, 5),
  param_specs = param_specs_gpd,
  mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 400, nburnin = 100, nchains = 1, thin = 2, seed = 1),
  mcmc_ps = list(niter = 400, nburnin = 100, nchains = 1, thin = 2, seed = 1)
)

bundle_sb_crp
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
CausalMixGPD causal bundle
PS model: Bayesian logit (A | X) 
      Field                Treated                    Control
    Backend Stick-Breaking Process Chinese Restaurant Process
     Kernel                amoroso                    amoroso
 Components                      5                          5
   GPD tail                   TRUE                       TRUE
    Epsilon                  0.025                      0.025

Outcome PS included: TRUE 
n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
summary(bundle_sb_crp)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
CausalMixGPD causal bundle summary
CausalMixGPD causal bundle
PS model: Bayesian logit (A | X) 
      Field                Treated                    Control
    Backend Stick-Breaking Process Chinese Restaurant Process
     Kernel                amoroso                    amoroso
 Components                      5                          5
   GPD tail                   TRUE                       TRUE
    Epsilon                  0.025                      0.025

Outcome PS included: TRUE 
n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit_sb_crp <- load_or_fit(
  "ex14-causal-different-backends-sb-fit_sb_crp",
  quiet_mcmc(dpmgpd.causal(bundle_sb_crp))
)
summary(fit_sb_crp)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
-- PS fit --
CausalMixGPD PS fit
model: logit 

-- Outcome fits --
[control]
MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Amoroso Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE
n = 232 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025
MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1 
Fit
Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.

[treated]
MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Amoroso Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE
n = 268 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025
MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1 
Fit
Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
params(fit_sb_crp)
```

## Model B: Treated CRP, Control SB (Amoroso + GPD)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
bundle_crp_sb <- bundle(
  x = y,
  treat = A,
  X = X,
  kernel = c("amoroso", "amoroso"),
  backend = c("crp", "sb"),
  PS = "logit",
  GPD = c(TRUE, TRUE),
  components = c(5, 5),
  param_specs = param_specs_gpd,
  mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 400, nburnin = 100, nchains = 1, thin = 2, seed = 2),
  mcmc_ps = list(niter = 400, nburnin = 100, nchains = 1, thin = 2, seed = 2)
)

bundle_crp_sb
```

   GPD tail                       TRUE                   TRUE
    Epsilon                      0.025                  0.025

Outcome PS included: TRUE 
n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268
